differential_diagnoses: Infectious etiology (e.g., fungal, Nocardia, or viral encephalitis)
next_steps: Obtain definitive histopathology (including immunohistochemistry for CD20, CD3, EBER/ISH, and GFAP)
Perform lumbar puncture for CSF cytology, flow cytometry, and protein/glucose analysis
Check serum for EBV DNA and autoimmune markers including ACE and vasculitis panel
Continue high-dose corticosteroids while awaiting permanent pathology sections
Consider stereotactic biopsy review by a neuropathologist specializing in neuro-oncology
Obtain whole-body PET/CT if lymphoma is confirmed to evaluate for systemic involvement

differential_diagnoses: Tumefactive Demyelinating Lesion (associated with autoimmune history)
Progressive Multifocal Leukoencephalopathy (PML) with atypical presentation
next_steps: Perform comprehensive immunohistochemical analysis of biopsy tissue including CD20, CD3, CD68, GFAP, EBER-ISH, and IgG4 staining
Review biopsy slides via expert neuropathology consultation at an academic referral center
Perform MR spectroscopy and perfusion imaging to evaluate for vascular/inflammatory versus neoplastic patterns
Order systemic EBV DNA PCR and serology
Perform a cautious lumbar puncture for CSF cytology, flow cytometry, protein, glucose, and meningitis/encephalitis infectious panels if clinically stable
Evaluate for occult systemic signs of lymphoproliferative disorder or inflammatory dermatosis
